tips.title: Starting the Discussion
tips.content: Take the initiative if you understand the topic well. Start with a solid definition or an impactful quote. It shows leadership.

tips.title: Adding Valuable Points
tips.content: Don't just agree or disagree. Say 'I agree, and to add to that...' or 'While that is true, we must also consider...'. Provide logical reasoning or statistics.

tips.title: Handling Interruptions
tips.content: Stay calm. If someone cuts you off, politely say 'Please let me finish my point, then you can add yours.' Don't raise your voice aggressively.

tips.title: Concluding Effectively
tips.content: Summarize the key takeaways naturally without presenting new information. A good summarizer shows excellent listening skills.

topics.title: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
points: AI is transforming industries like healthcare, finance, and education.
It increases efficiency by automating repetitive tasks.
AI improves decision-making using data analysis.
However, it may lead to job displacement in some sectors.
AI-powered tools like chatbots enhance customer service.
Ethical concerns include bias and lack of transparency.
AI is crucial for future technologies like self-driving cars.
Governments need regulations for responsible AI use.
Overall, AI is powerful but must be used responsibly.

topics.title: Social Media — Boon or Bane
points: It helps in sharing information quickly.
Useful for education and awareness campaigns.
Impacts mental health negatively in many cases.
Helps businesses in marketing and branding.
Balance in usage is very important.

topics.title: Online Education VS Offline Education
points: Offline education builds discipline and routine.
Both have pros and cons depending on context.

topics.title: Is India Ready for Digital Economy?
points: India is rapidly growing in digital infrastructure.
Rural areas still lack proper access.
Government initiatives like Digital India help growth.
Digital literacy is still low in some regions.
India is progressing but needs improvement.

topics.title: Work From Home — Good or Bad
points: Can reduce productivity in some cases.
Leads to isolation and less teamwork.
Suitable for IT and tech roles.
Depends on job type and individual discipline.

topics.title: Impact of Technology on Jobs
points: AI and ML are reshaping industries.
Overall impact is both positive and challenging.

topics.title: Should Students Focus on Skills or Marks?
points: Skills are more important in real-world jobs.
Marks help in shortlisting during placements.
Practical knowledge matters more than theory.
Skills help in long-term career growth.
Final success depends on ability, not just marks.

topics.title: Startups VS MNCs
points: Salary may be higher in MNCs initially.

topics.title: Role of Youth in Nation Building
points: Youth are the backbone of a nation.
Should focus on education and skills.
Youth participation in politics is increasing.
They shape the future of the country.

topics.title: Climate Change
points: Climate change is a global issue.
Caused by pollution and human activities.

dialogue.speaker: Candidate A (Initiator)
dialogue.text: Good morning everyone. The topic for our discussion is 'Is Data the new Oil?'. Much like oil drove the industrial economy, data is undoubtedly driving the digital economy today. Companies are becoming trillion-dollar entities purely based on data utilization.

dialogue.speaker: Candidate B
dialogue.text: I agree with Candidate A. For example, personalized recommendations on Netflix or Amazon are driven by our data. It fuels their revenue.

dialogue.speaker: Candidate C
dialogue.text: While that's true, we must also note a key difference. Oil is a finite resource, whereas data is infinitely generated. Oil diminishes when consumed, but data multiplies as we use it.

dialogue.speaker: Candidate A
dialogue.text: That's an excellent point, Candidate C. Furthermore, unrefined oil is useless, and raw data is similarly useless. It needs processing and analytics to have value, much like oil needs refining.

dialogue.speaker: Candidate D (Summarizer)
dialogue.text: To summarize our discussion, we agree that data parallels oil as an economic driver and requires 'refining' through analytics to be valuable. However, unlike oil, its infinite nature makes it an even more powerful asset for the future.
